description_long: This is a rustic cabin elevated on stilts, featuring a thatched gable roof with deep overhangs and a prominent front porch accessed by a wooden staircase. The exterior walls are constructed from natural materials, likely wood or bamboo, and the roof is made of layered thatch, giving it a traditional, rural appearance. The cabin is divided into two main sections, with the right section extending forward to form the porch area. There are visible doorways with simple coverings, and the structure is supported by slender posts. The porch is open and partially sheltered by the roof, providing a transitional space between the outdoors and the interior. The overall design suggests a lightweight, ventilated structure suitable for warm climates and rural or semi-urban settings.
description: A rustic, stilted cabin with a thatched gable roof, open front porch, and natural wood or bamboo construction. The structure features deep roof overhangs and simple doorways, evoking a traditional rural style.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open porch, wooden stairs, and main entrance beneath the thatched roof overhang.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof, the side doorway, and the stilted support structure.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back wall, absence of windows, and the two-sectioned roof layout.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a clear look at the side entrance and the extended roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: gable thatched roof with deep overhangs and layered natural material
attribute_window_arrangement: No visible windows on any side; the cabin relies on doorways for ventilation and light.
attribute_door_placement: Two exterior doors: one centered on the front porch and one on the left side wall, both with simple coverings.
